events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: residential water heater, closed system, no expansion tank. Work in-progress. IPC 504 and IRC P2804 require thermal expansion control on closed systems. No expansion tank means non-compliant and potential pressure hazard. Severity: medium (could lead to pressure relief valve dripping, potential tank failure). Decision: fail. Risk: medium. Component conditions: non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 60. Confidence high.

    "No thermal expansion tank installed on a closed water heater system",
    "Closed system lacks required pressure relief for thermal expansion, risking T&P valve weeping and pressure buildup"

  "actions": [
    "Install an approved expansion tank sized to the water heater capacity and system pressure",
    "Verify system pressure and T&P valve operation after expansion tank installation",
    "Do not place system into normal service until expansion control is installed"
  ],
  "rationale": "On a closed system, heated water has nowhere to expand, so code requires a thermal expansion tank or equivalent approved device. Its absence is a non-compliant installation that can cause excessive pressure, T&P valve discharge, and potential component damage.",
